statutory_basis: Federal Constitution Article 132(1)(d) for service classification

rule_set.text_or_description: MACC investigates and refers cases to the Attorney-General's Chambers, which decides whether to charge. MACC cannot prosecute directly.
rule_set.legal_source: MACC Act 2009, Sections 23-29; Federal Constitution Article 145(3)
rule_set.enforcement_mechanism: Statutory referral to AGC; AGC discretion under Article 145(3)

rule_set.text_or_description: MACC Chief Commissioner appointed by YDPA on PM advice for renewable terms; reports to Prime Minister's Department.
rule_set.enforcement_mechanism: Statutory; tenure renewal at PM discretion

rule_set.text_or_description: Tenure-extension practice: MACC chief tenure has been extended by sitting PM rather than fixed term, reducing institutional independence in practice.

Five oversight committees (Anti-Corruption Advisory Board, Special Committee on Corruption, Complaints Committee, Operations Review Panel, Consultation and Prevention of Corruption Panel) provide layered review but have advisory powers only.
rule_set.enforcement_mechanism: Statutory; committees report to Parliament annually

notes: Predecessors: Badan Pencegah Rasuah / Anti-Corruption Agency (1967-2008). The 2009 reorganisation gave statutory independence but kept the chief's tenure at PM discretion. The structural tension between investigative independence and tenure-controlled appointment is the single most consequential weakness in the anti-corruption architecture.
